rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by adding or modifying AGREE annex clauses in the correct scope
Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex
Align helper declaration types with expression types
Remove clauses that compare opaque data ranges or Boolean values unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar
generic_rules: Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value; for range comparisons
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers, data types, or property references that are absent from the analyzed model and its configured dependencies
anti_patterns: Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not add architecture to fix unresolved references; instead, trace and fix the root unresolved reference or delete the invalid clause
irrelevant_topics: Byte_Count (property association) does not point to anything
